factors.summary: Overall demand is decelerating, particularly in the housing market.

evidence.text: According to the U.S. Census Bureau, actual U.S. total housing starts were 0.4 million for the second quarter of 2023, a decrease of 11.3% compared to the second quarter of 2022.

factors.summary: Order backlog or book-to-bill is not explicitly addressed in the provided text.

evidence.text: No specific information on order backlog or book-to-bill trends was found in the text.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing commodity price deflation, indicating pressure on pricing power.

evidence.text: Our net sales for the second quarter of 2023 decreased 34.6% from the same period last year. The decrease was driven by a decrease in core organic sales of 22.3%, primarily in lumber and lumber sheet goods, with commodity price deflation accounting for another 16.2%

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin value-add products, which is beneficial.

evidence.text: This increase was driven by improved product mix toward our value-add products primarily attributable to our recent strategic investments in multi-family value-add operations as well as relative sales strength of all value-add operations against decreased housing starts.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating due to declining housing starts and economic conditions.

evidence.text: Our net sales for the second quarter of 2023 decreased 34.6% from the same period last year. The decrease was driven by a decrease in core organic sales of 22.3%, primarily in lumber and lumber sheet goods, with commodity price deflation accounting for another 16.2%

factors.summary: Unit volumes are declining across all product categories due to a slowing economy and declining housing starts.

evidence.text: We experienced decreased net sales in all of our product categories primarily due to a slowing economy, a decline in core organic sales as a result of declining housing starts, and commodity deflation.

factors.summary: The housing market is slowing, particularly in single-family homes, while multi-family segments show relative strength.

evidence.text: Actual U.S. single-family starts for the second quarter of 2023 were 0.3 million, a decrease of 13.8% compared to the second quarter of 2022. [...] Our net sales for the second quarter of 2023 decreased 34.6% from the same period last year. [...] This increase was driven by improved product mix toward our value-add products primarily attributable to our recent strategic investments in multi-family value-add operations as well as relative sales strength of all value-add operations against decreased housing starts.

factors.summary: Commodity prices are declining, particularly in lumber, which is affecting revenue.

evidence.text: The decrease was driven by a decrease in core organic sales of 22.3%, primarily in lumber and lumber sheet goods, with commodity price deflation accounting for another 16.2%

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable, with no significant pressure mentioned in the text.

evidence.text: No specific mention of labor cost pressures or wage inflation impacting margins was found in the text.

factors.summary: Gross margins are improving slightly, while operating margins are under pressure due to higher SG&A expenses.

evidence.text: Gross margin percentage increased by 0.4% compared to the second quarter of 2022 [...] Selling, general and administrative expenses, as a percentage of net sales, were 22.5% in the second quarter of 2023, a 7.4% increase from 15.1% in the second quarter of 2022

factors.summary: Cost reduction efforts are ongoing, particularly in variable compensation, but the benefits are offset by higher amortization and operating expenses from acquisitions.

evidence.text: Selling, general and administrative expenses decreased by $28.4 million, or 2.7%, primarily due to decreased variable compensation as a result of decreased sales, partially offset by higher amortization expense and additional operating expenses from locations acquired within the last twelve months.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2023 Compared with the Three Months Ended June 30, 2022

factors.summary: The company has significant indebtedness, but it is managing liquidity through borrowings and cash on hand.

evidence.text: Our Revolving Facility will be primarily used for working capital, general corporate purposes and funding capital expenditures and potential future growth opportunities. [...] As of June 30, 2023, we had $1.0 billion in outstanding borrowings under our Revolving Facility, and our net excess borrowing availability was $0.8 billion after being reduced by outstanding letters of credit totaling $70.4 million.

evidence.text: During the six months ended June 30, 2023, the Company repurchased 14.5 million shares at a weighted average price of $93.02 per share, for a total cost of approximately $1.4 billion, inclusive of fees and taxes.
